revision.comment: add in the dumb navF styles so we can maybe remove them at some point for mw-collapsible
give the toggle a little margin
add a little top margin per tper

Protected "[[Module:Sidebar/styles.css]]": [[Wikipedia:High-risk templates|High-risk template or module]] ([[User:MusikBot II/TemplateProtector|more info]]) ([Edit=Require template editor access] (indefinite) [Move=Require template editor access] (indefinite))
add css to force display on everything that hits nomobile
remove sidebar collapsible-toggle manipulation, add sidebar-list-title-c
add border-collapse, probably break all the sidebars... but it's the behavior to expect when this gets turned into divs
wraplinks work like it used to
ditch some weird padding in a collapse version padding
a duplicate to the style just above? (0.2em 0.8em looks better, imo)
use --background-color-neutral-subtle (with fallback) as background, for dark mode compatibility
Allow templates such as [[Elections_in_India]] to opt out of night mode styles via a notheme class.
use border-color-base (#a2a9b1) for consistency and better appearance in dark mode
move @print none to templatestyles from [[MediaWiki:Print.css]] to support [[MediaWiki talk:Common.css/to do#Turn mobile.css/js totally off]]
actually, doesn't need to be high specificity
[[mw:Recommendations_for_night_mode_compatibility_on_Wikimedia_wikis]] Improve for dark mode (fixes [[2024_Democratic_Party_presidential_primaries]]) and removes styles from print stylesheet
(Defensively add color white important to make sure these do not get mixed)
-sidebar-navbar entirely; upstream takes care of these
ok I lied that makes sense to have since sidebars can be in other namespaces
1 revision imported: reimporting to fix errors

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported: lets hope this fixes it since the qq=draft was showing up
